# Rixey Manor

> A historic 1801 estate wedding venue in Rixeyville, Culpeper County, Northern Virginia.
> One wedding per weekend, exclusive use, up to 250 guests.
> 60 miles from Washington DC.

## About

- Built in 1801. Restored and operated by Isadora Martin-Dye since 2014.
- Originally a working farmhouse called Pleasant Hill (not a plantation manor). The grand Classical Revival columned front was added in 1928 for the wedding of James Marion Dyer and Margaret Robinson Lewis, who owned the house and still marked their 50th anniversary on the same steps in 1978. Full history: rixeymanor.com/history
- The estate mostly passed down through the women of the Rixey family and stayed in the family into the mid-1990s. It is woman-owned again today (Isadora Martin-Dye).
- Like every estate of its age in Virginia, it was built on the labour of enslaved people. The family who held it were farmers and Confederates who enslaved people (one documented: a craftsman named Henry). Rixey Manor names this openly, is researching to recover the names of the enslaved, and is today an inclusive venue that welcomes every couple regardless of race, religion, or orientation.
- Located in Rixeyville, Culpeper County, Northern Virginia (Rappahannock River region).
- 30 acres including Rixey Lake and Blue Ridge Mountain views.
- The Knot Hall of Fame. 220+ five-star reviews.
- No required vendor list. BYOB. Pet friendly. ADA accessible (main floor).
- Military and first responder discount: 10%.
- Elopement packages: from $950, Mon–Wed, up to 12 guests.

## Spaces

- Lake View Ceremony Site (outdoor, up to 250 guests)
- The Ballroom (indoor, climate controlled, up to 100 guests, ADA accessible)
- The Terrace / Patio (outdoor, tent option, up to 200+ guests, ADA accessible)
- The Rooftop (1,800 sq ft, panoramic Blue Ridge views, staircase access only)
- 4 manor bedrooms + Blacksmith Cottage (overnight lodging, up to 14 guests)

## Packages & Pricing

Three wedding packages. Two-tier season: off-season is January, February, July, August;
peak is everything else (March through June, September through December).

- The Estate Weekend (Friday 3pm to Sunday 10am — brunch upgrade extends check-out
  to 1pm — includes Friday rehearsal dinner space and two nights of lodging for up
  to 14 guests): off-season $16,000, peak $21,000
- The Wedding Day (Saturday only, 8am to 10pm, no rehearsal dinner, overnights
  available as upgrades): off-season $12,000, peak $15,500
- The Midweek Wedding (Tuesday or Wednesday, 8am to 9pm, no overnights):
  off-season $7,000, peak $9,000
- Elopement: from $950, Mon–Wed, up to 12 guests
- Saturday guests over 100: +$1,500 per 50 guests (max 200)
- Friday rehearsal dinner guests over 50 (Estate Weekend only): +$1,500 per 50 (max 150)

Stackable discounts (capped at 20% total): off-site ceremony 5%, recommended
vendors only 5%, under 50 Saturday guests 10%, military / veteran / first
responder 10%.

## What's Included With Every Package

- Full exclusive use of the property for the booked period (Wedding Day = totally
  private all day; Estate Weekend = whole weekend, no other weddings)
- On-site Rixey coordinator (planning through wedding day)
- Day-of venue team
- Licensed in-house bartending, up to 6 hours of bar service
- BYOB with no corkage fees
- Basic table linens
- Silk floral and candle centerpiece package
- Chiavari chairs and tables (up to 200 guests)
- Borrow shed (décor library: vases, candles, lanterns, signs)
- Two getting-ready spaces (manor + Blacksmith Cottage)
- No required vendor list
- No vendor markup or referral commission on outside vendors
- No day-of liability insurance required from the couple
- Outside wedding planner policy: external planners must be approved
  before the contract is signed, must have planned at least 5 weddings,
  and must carry their own liability insurance. Planners hired after
  the contract is signed must come from the Rixey recommended planner
  list (available on request) and must also carry insurance.

## Inclusion (rixeymanor.com/inclusion)

Rixey Manor names four communities in every contract. The protections
and adaptations apply to the couple AND to their family, wedding
party and guests — not just the contracting couple:

- LGBTQ+ couples and families: welcomed and protected by name. Partner
  1 / Partner 2 on intake forms; couple's suite (not bridal suite);
  wedding party (not bridal party); the names, pronouns and roles that
  matter (the couple's, the wedding party's, the family's) briefed to
  every vendor in advance.
- Disabled couples and couples with disabilities: ballroom, main-floor
  restrooms, patio, and ceremony lawn are step-free and ADA-compliant.
  Accessible parking adjacent to the entrance. Upstairs bedrooms and
  rooftop are stairs only and named upfront. Service animals welcome.
  Coordinator walk-through offered to any couple, family member,
  wedding-party member or guest in advance.
- Couples with chronic illness or ongoing medical needs (and the same
  for medical needs in the wedding party or family): private space
  reserved for the whole day at no additional charge, food and
  medication windows on the day-of timeline, couple-controlled
  disclosure to guests, vendors and family.
- Neurodivergent couples (Autistic, ADHD, AuDHD, sensory-sensitive,
  anxious). The commitment is to listen and adapt to whatever reduces
  anxiety for the couple, the wedding party and the guests — not to
  apply a universal template. Possible adaptations include agendas
  ahead of planning meetings, written summaries after conversations,
  communication synced across text and email, DJ lighting reviewed in
  advance, a quiet space held open throughout the event, built-in
  breaks across the day, and a pre-agreed pause-or-end signal honoured
  without discussion. Which of these are used depends on what the
  couple and the people standing closest to them actually need.

Every Rixey contract carries a zero-tolerance anti-hate-speech and
anti-harassment clause covering sexual orientation, gender identity
and expression, race, religion, disability, national origin, age, and
pregnancy. Plus an anti-abuse clause protecting every couple and
guest on the property.
- Overnight accommodation for up to 14 (Estate Weekend only; Wedding Day add-on)
- Pets welcome at the ceremony
- No guest minimum
- Parking on-site, Wi-Fi throughout the manor
- No cake-cutting fee, no service charge added at the end

## Location

- Address: 9155 Pleasant Hill Lane, Rixeyville, VA 22737
- County: Culpeper County, Northern Virginia
- 60 miles from Washington DC, Charlottesville, Fredericksburg, and Middleburg
- Near Shenandoah Valley and Blue Ridge Mountains

## Contact

- Phone: (540) 212-4545 (calls and texts accepted)
- Email: info@rixeymanor.com
- Website: https://www.rixeymanor.com
